question_text: Let $X _ { 1 } , X _ { 2 } , X _ { 3 } , X _ { 4 }$ be a sample of size 4 from Bernoulli(θ), $0 < \theta < 1$ . We test $\begin{array} { r } { H _ { 0 } : \theta = \frac { 1 } { 4 } } \end{array}$ vs $\textstyle H _ { 1 } : \theta > { \frac { 1 } { 4 } }$ , rejecting $H _ { 0 }$ if $X _ { 1 } + X _ { 2 } + X _ { 3 } + X _ { 4 } > 2$ . If α is the Type-I error and $\gamma ( \theta )$ the power function, then find $1 6 \alpha + 7 \gamma \left( { \frac { 1 } { 2 } } \right)$ (in integer).
solution:
Let $Y = X _ { 1 } + X _ { 2 } + X _ { 3 } + X _ { 4 } \sim$ Binomial(4, θ). Reject $H _ { 0 }$ when $Y > 2 , \mathrm { i . e . } Y = 3 \mathrm { o r } 4 .$

Type-I error:

 $ \alpha = P ( Y > 2 \mid \theta = { \frac { 1 } { 4 } } ) = { \binom { 4 } { 3 } } { \binom { 1 } { 4 } } ^ { 3 } { \binom { 3 } { 4 } } + { \binom { 4 } { 4 } } { \binom { 1 } { 4 } } ^ { 4 } = 4 { \binom { 1 } { 6 4 } } { \binom { 3 } { 4 } } + { \frac { 1 } { 2 5 6 } } = { \frac { 1 3 } { 2 5 6 } } \approx 0 . 0 5 0 8 . $ 

Power at $\theta = { \frac { 1 } { 2 } }$

 $ \gamma \big ( { \textstyle { \frac { 1 } { 2 } } } \big ) = P ( Y > 2 \mid \theta = { \textstyle { \frac { 1 } { 2 } } } ) = { \binom { 4 } { 3 } } \left( { \textstyle { \frac { 1 } { 2 } } } \right) ^ { 3 } \big ( { \textstyle { \frac { 1 } { 2 } } } \big ) + { \binom { 4 } { 4 } } \left( { \textstyle { \frac { 1 } { 2 } } } \right) ^ { 4 } = 0 . 3 1 2 5 . $ 

Hence

 $ 1 6 \alpha + 7 \gamma \left( { \textstyle { \frac { 1 } { 2 } } } \right) = 1 6 ( 0 . 0 5 0 8 ) + 7 ( 0 . 3 1 2 5 ) = 0 . 8 1 2 8 + 2 . 1 8 7 5 = 3 . 0 0 0 3 \approx \left[ 3 \right] . $ 
Quick Tip

For small samples of Bernoulli data, the power is computed from Binomial probabilities under each parameter value.
